channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Lenovo to acquire Infinidat to expand its storage folio
item.description: Lenovo Group has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Tel Aviv-based enterprise storage provider Infinidat, for an undisclosed sum, to expand its storage offerings. The startup provides several offerings, such as InfiniBox, Infinibox SSA, InfiniGuard, and InfuzeOS.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google Unveils New Code Migration Process Using AI
item.description: Google has introduced a new step-by-step process and common toolkit that utilizes large language models (LLMs) to accelerate code migrations, achieving a 50% time reduction in end-to-end work.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Biden’s clean AI infrastructure plan could be hanging by a thread
item.description: An executive order from outgoing US president Joe Biden aimed at advancing the nation’s leadership in AI infrastructure is “welcome and needed in theory, but this one seems rushed and somewhat at odds with the order just passed on AI chip export restrictions,” an analyst said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: President Biden’s new executive order aims to bolster national security
item.description: President Joe Biden issued an executive order on Thursday aimed at strengthening the nation’s cybersecurity and making it easier to go after foreign adversaries or hacking groups that try to compromise U.S. internet and telecommunication systems.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Biden's new executive order aims to shore up US cyber defenses
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden issued an executive order Thursday aimed at strengthening the nation's cybersecurity and making it easier to go after foreign adversaries or hacking groups that try to compromise U.S. internet and telecommunication systems.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Drone collides with paraglider, forcing emergency landing
item.description: The incident happened the same day that a firefighting aircraft had to be grounded while battling the Palisades Fire.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, NewsNation

item.title: Avery label maker confirms attack on its site, customer credit card info stolen
item.description: Avery was unknowingly hosting a credit card skimmer for half a year, exposing customer data to hackers.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia’s new next-gen GPU benchmarks cause concern among PC gamers, particularly with the RTX 5080 – but don’t panic yet
item.description: Not convinced RTX 5000 is a winner? Folks worried about a reliance on ‘fake frames’ may not be that impressed with these fresh benchmarks.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Substack is trying to court displaced TikTok creators
item.description: “We’re going to rescue the smart people from TikTok!” Substack CEO Chris Best wrote in a Note posted Monday. If you’ve been on your Substack newsfeed lately, you’ve likely seen the platform rolling out a warm welcome to TikTok users, as the days count down to a potential TikTok ban in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC posts 57% surge in profit thanks to the AI boom
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Taiwan computer chip maker TSMC reported Thursday that its profit in the last quarter rose 57%, buoyed by the artificial intelligence boom. The world’s biggest semiconductor manufacturer reported a net profit of 374.7 billion new Taiwan dollars ($11.4 billion), higher than expected.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Joe Biden going out same way as Aaron Rodgers
item.description: Bill O'Reilly believes outgoing president Joe Biden is "going out the same way" as New York Jets’ starting quarterback Aaron Rodgers.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Biden won't enforce TikTok ban, official says, leaving fate of app to Trump
item.description: President Joe Biden won’t enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office, a U.S. official says.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: SpaceX launches U.S and Japanese landers to the moon
item.description: In a two-for-one moonshot, SpaceX launched a pair of lunar landers Wednesday for U.S. and Japanese companies looking to jump-start business on Earth’s dusty sidekick.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Fox will stream next month's Super Bowl for free on Tubi
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fox will stream the Super Bowl on Tubi for the first time, giving streamers and cord cutters a chance to watch the big game on Feb. 9 for free. Tubi was acquired by Fox Corporation in 2020. It is a free, ad-supported streaming service.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden's big semiconductor law will ramp up US chip production -- but at a high cost, report finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A sweeping 2022 law, touted by President Joe Biden as a way to revive U.S. manufacturing of semiconductors and reduce the country’s reliance on foreign-made computer chips, will “sharply increase production’’ of semiconductors in the United States.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: A CSO’s perspective: 8 cyber predictions for 2025
item.description: As we step into 2025, the cyberthreat landscape is once again more dynamic and challenging than the year before. In 2024, we witnessed a remarkable acceleration in cyberattacks of all types, many fueled by advancements in generative AI. For security leaders, the stakes are higher than ever.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Smart glasses’ appeal comes into focus at CES 2025
item.description: Smart glasses attracted a lot of attention at last week’s Consumer Electronics Show, with a range of devices on display that combine lightweight frames with functionality such as heads-up displays and AI-powered assistants.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Ericsson unveils genAI assistant for 5G network operations
item.description: Telecommunications and networking provider Ericsson recently launched its generative AI-based virtual assistant that uses large language model (LLM) technology to read, understand, and generate new content to provide personalized answers for network operators configuring wireless 5G networks, troubl
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: HPE Lands $1 Billion Deal with X for AI Servers
item.description: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has signed a contract exceeding $1 billion to provide AI servers for X, the platform formerly known as Twitter.
item.dc:creator: Network World
